name: Adipose MSC exosomes had exploratory Alzheimer's cognition signal
summary: A nine-participant open-label phase I/II Alzheimer's trial supported intranasal exosome tolerability and generated exploratory medium-dose cognitive signals without biomarker separation.
statement: In nine patients with mild to moderate Alzheimer's disease, intranasal allogenic adipose MSC-derived exosomes were reported as well tolerated; all-subject cognition changes at 12 weeks were not significant, while the medium-dose arm showed within-arm ADAS-cog and MoCA-B improvement trends.
population_or_model: Adults at least 50 years old with mild to moderate Alzheimer's disease
time_horizon: Twenty-four intranasal administrations over 12 weeks with follow-up to 48 weeks
quantitative_note: The medium-dose arm reported ADAS-cog -2.33 and MoCA-B +2.38 at week 12, with ADAS-cog -3.98 by week 36; amyloid and tau deposition did not differ significantly among dose arms.
caveats: The trial was open-label, non-randomized, single-center, and very small.
No placebo or standard-care control supports causal efficacy inference.
The signal is Alzheimer's disease-specific and does not establish broad aging or LEV-relevant benefit.
